title: calamagrostis autumnalis subsp. microtis
intro: Calamagrostis autumnalis subsp. microtis, also known as small-spiked meadow-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calamagrostis autumnalis subsp. microtis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. It can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
